content.title: Example 1 — Cross-Platform UI (Java)
content.body: A desktop app renders native UI components on Windows and macOS. The `Application` client is injected with a `GUIFactory` and never calls `new` on any concrete product.

content.title: Cross-Platform UI
Example 2 — Multi-Cloud Provisioning (Java)
content.body: A `DeploymentManager` client provisions infrastructure on AWS or GCP. `EC2Instance` must never be paired with `GoogleCloudStorage` — the factory structure makes this impossible.

content.title: Multi-Cloud Provisioning
Example 3 — Global E-Commerce Compliance (Java)
content.body: Three products per family with real business logic. EU uses VAT (20%) + SEPA + GDPR logging. NA uses Sales Tax (7%) + Stripe + SOX auditing. Mixing them produces legally invalid transactions.

content.title: E-Commerce Compliance
Example 4 — Graphics API (Java)
content.body: A 3D game engine prevents DirectX/Vulkan crashes. A DirectX shader must never be paired with a Vulkan texture.

content.title: Graphics API

flashcards.q: In the E-Commerce example, why is mixing EU and NA products a 'catastrophic legal failure'?
flashcards.a: EU uses VAT (20%), SEPA payments, and GDPR logging; NA uses Sales Tax (7%), Stripe payments, and SOX auditing. Pairing an EU tax calculator with NA compliance logging produces legally invalid transactions.

flashcards.q: Can a Concrete Factory return existing (cached) objects instead of creating new ones?
flashcards.a: Yes. The factory method can return objects from a cache or object pool. The client doesn't know or care whether it received a new or recycled instance — it interacts via the abstract interface.

problems.q: **Theme System (Medium):** A mobile app supports Light and Dark themes. Each theme provides a `Background` (with `render()`) and a `TextLabel` (with `display(String text)`). Implement the full Abstract Factory pattern. The `Screen` client takes a factory via constructor and has a `draw()` method. Switching themes requires changing exactly one line.
problems.hint: The Screen constructor takes a ThemeFactory. draw() calls render() and display(). The only change needed to switch themes is which factory you pass in at initialization.
